assumptions_or_open_questions: The nix-shell Rust toolchain (cargo 1.86.0, rustc stable) is a valid compilation and test environment for this codebase — confirmed by successful cargo check and cargo test --lib with all 86 tests passing.
The single compiler warning (unused variable `spawned` in conformance_spec/runner.rs:108) is pre-existing and unrelated to the artifact-metadata fix.

The existing two regression tests (service-level divergent-adapter test and final_review e2e persistence test) provide sufficient coverage given that completion.rs and prompt_review.rs use the identical envelope-metadata pattern and the full test suite exercises those code paths without failure.
data.problem_framing: Cycle 3 implementation and all review stages conditionally approved the artifact-metadata fix, pending executable validation via cargo check and cargo test. The Rust toolchain was unavailable in prior stages. This planning round addresses the two pending amendments: (1) run cargo check and the two named regression tests, and (2) assess whether additional completion/prompt-review persistence tests are needed. The Rust toolchain is now available via nix-shell, and all validation has been completed successfully: cargo check passes, both regression tests pass, and the full 86-test lib suite passes with zero failures.

proposed_work.details: cargo check passes (compilation verified). cargo test --lib invoke_preserves_adapter_reported_backend_and_model_metadata passes (service-level regression). cargo test --lib persisted_artifacts_use_envelope_metadata_not_resolved_target passes (e2e artifact persistence regression). Full test suite: 86 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored. Both pending amendments from acceptance_qa cycle 3 round 1 are satisfied. The optional second amendment (broader acceptance coverage for completion and prompt-review paths) is adequately covered by the existing test suite passing — those paths use the same envelope-metadata pattern verified by the final_review test and exercised by the full suite.
proposed_work.summary: No further implementation work required — all pending amendments are resolved

risks: No remaining risks. All prior conditional approvals cited Rust toolchain unavailability as the sole blocker; that blocker is now resolved with all tests passing.
